title: The Four Oxen and the Lion
lines: Four Oxen lived together in a meadow and were great friends.
They would stand together in the center of the field, facing outward.
A Lion, hungry and fierce, came to the meadow, but he could not approach them.
'If I can make them quarrel," thought the Lion, 'I will be able to catch one.'
So he whispered accusations to each Ox, saying the others did not really like him.
Soon the Oxen began to quarrel and separated.
Once they were apart, the Lion caught and ate them one by one.
moral: United we stand, divided we fall.
